data.title: Rimini Street: Litigation Overhang Removed but Modest Growth Keeps Shares Fairly Valued at Hold
data.description:
Rimini Street, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Derrick Wood from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $4.25 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derrick Wood has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Rimini Street’s solid fourth quarter, improving remaining performance obligations, and management’s confidence in sales momentum supported by plans to expand sales capacity and increase quotas. At the same time, overall revenue growth remains modest, with certain regions and product lines showing flat or negative trends, and customer metrics such as retention and net new clients reflecting only incremental progress rather than a clear inflection.

Rimini Street now operates without the prior litigation overhang and is pursuing growth initiatives like its partnership with ServiceNow and Agentic AI for ERP, which could support better performance in the second half and beyond. However, despite an undemanding valuation on a free cash flow basis, Wood views the shares as fairly valued until the company can demonstrate a more sustained and meaningful reacceleration of growth, leading him to maintain a Hold rating rather than move to a more aggressive stance.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Telix: Dual-Product PSMA Strategy and Cash-Generating Diagnostics Base Support Buy Rating and Multibillion-Dollar Therapeutics Upside
data.description:
Telix Pharmaceuticals, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Tara Bancroft from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a A$20.00 price target.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tara Bancroft has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Telix’s strong growth outlook and expanding diagnostic franchise. He highlights that management’s 2026 revenue guidance of $950–970MM implies high‑teens to low‑20s percent growth and already comes in about 3% above Street expectations, supported by robust momentum in precision diagnostics and a full year of RLS contribution.

He also points to Telix’s dual‑product PSMA PET strategy with Illuccix and Gozellix, which is designed to capture a wider set of payors through differentiated reimbursement structures, thereby sustaining roughly 25% annual growth in diagnostic sales. In Bancroft’s view, this cash‑generating diagnostics base can be reinvested into an advancing therapeutics pipeline targeting a multibillion‑dollar market opportunity, creating an attractive risk‑reward profile that underpins his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on February 8, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$34.00 price target.

data.title: Bicara: Best-in-Class Potential for Ficerafusp Alfa in First-Line HPV-Negative Head and Neck Cancer Supports Buy Rating and $30 Target
data.description:
Analyst Soumit Roy from JonesTrading maintained a Buy rating on Bicara Therapeutics Inc. and keeping the price target at $30.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Soumit Roy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore Bicara’s competitive positioning in first-line HPV‑negative head and neck cancer. The latest MHNCS data show that ficerafusp alfa at the selected 1,500 mg weekly dose plus Keytruda delivers efficacy broadly comparable to the higher 2,000 mg every‑two‑weeks dose, while supporting a more convenient loading and less frequent maintenance schedule pending regulatory feedback.

Soumit Roy’s rating is based on the drug’s strong response depth, encouraging durability of benefit, and manageable safety profile at the chosen regimen, alongside pharmacokinetic data indicating exposure levels similar to the higher dose. Importantly, Bicara’s candidate appears to outperform J&J’s Rybrevant plus Keytruda in a comparable population on complete response rate, progression‑free survival, and duration of response, supporting a best‑in‑class narrative and justifying a $30 price target and a Buy recommendation.
